summary: Iteration 3 full-stack review: multilingual handover detection, staff display name, admin heartbeat, offline message flow, refund knowledge, AI Inbox UI. 25/25 backend tests pass. Landing page 'Live Chat?' label, AIWidget offline form (all 4 testids), handover transfer message, and Admin AI Inbox testids (staff-name-input, staff-name-save, handover-waiting-badge, offline-msgs-toggle, offline-messages-list, Wants Staff badge) are all verified end-to-end through the public preview URL.

minor.issue: No explicit 90s-stale admin_online path exercised in automated tests due to CI time budget; verified via clearing last_admin_seen in Mongo and re-polling — admin_online flipped to false. Recommend adding a debug hook for clock-injection in the future.
Email is silently lowercased before persistence. Not a bug but worth documenting in API reference so admins know lookups must use lowercase.
No pagination; to_list(200) cap will quietly drop older messages once exceeded. Consider added skip/limit params.

issues: Third-party tawk.to embed is blocked by CORS (console error). It's disabled/blocked in preview env but still attempted — consider guarding the injection behind an env flag to suppress noisy console errors.

action_items: (Optional) Suppress tawk.to embed in preview env or guard behind REACT_APP_* flag to clean up console errors.
(Optional) Add pagination + status filter to /api/ai/admin/offline-messages for longer-term scalability.
(Docs) Note that offline-message email is stored lowercase.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_and_chat.py admin_ai_release updates status -> 'ai' but does NOT call $unset on last_admin_seen, which is correct. OK.

admin_ai_send() auto-refreshes last_admin_seen heartbeat when admin replies — good UX, but means an admin reply alone (no explicit heartbeat) keeps admin_online=true. This is intentional and documented as 'sending implicitly takes over'. OK.

ai_chat() detection: relies on the LLM emitting the literal HANDOVER_REQUEST marker. All 4 tested languages (en/de/es/fr) succeeded in a single run, but because the trigger is model-driven it may have occasional misses. Consider adding a deterministic regex fallback for phrases like 'staff|agent|human|Mitarbeiter|agente|humano' as a safety net.
ai_chat() truncates user text to [:2000] and assistant to [:4000] — good.

admin_ai_sessions() computes handover_waiting over the 100-item window only; if waiting sessions fall outside the most recent 100 by last_activity they won't be counted. Edge-case but should use a direct count query: db.ai_sessions.count_documents({'needs_handover': True, 'status': {'$ne': 'human'}}).
seed_data_creation: Test created ~6 TEST_ prefixed offline messages and ~10 ai_sessions with needs_handover=true during the handover multilingual run. Offline messages remain in db.ai_offline_messages (email prefix 'test_offline_' / 'test_ui_offline'). Ai_settings.last_admin_seen was cleared during testing (admin_online=false) — it gets restored on the next admin heartbeat automatically from the Admin panel.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_3_features.py -v` from /app for regression. BASE_URL is derived from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Note: TestAdminHeartbeat.test_admin_online_flips_false_after_stale does NOT wait 90s; it only asserts the field is a bool. To truly test the offline-side, clear ai_settings.last_admin_seen directly in Mongo (as we did) and re-poll. The Admin panel's AIInboxPanel testids: staff-name-input, staff-name-save, handover-waiting-badge, offline-msgs-toggle, offline-messages-list, plus 'Wants Staff' / 'Leave Chat' text markers. 'Leave Chat' only renders when a session.status==human is selected in the right-pane.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. The two initial offline-message test failures were due to the test using a mixed-case email while the backend normalizes to lowercase before persistence. Test was updated to use lowercase; now passing.
